He remembers the pain and fear in his community in the eastern Congo city of Beni during the 2018-2020 Ebola outbreak, history's second-biggest with more than 3,400 reported cases and over 2,200 deaths.

video:video.video:title: Northern English town set for by-election which may give Andy Burnham a path to be next UK leader
video:video.video:description: Nearly 75,000 voters in Ashton-in-Makerfield will cast ballots in a contest that may well pick the UK’s next prime minister, or plunge Britain's febrile politics into even more turmoil.

video:video.video:description: Damaging storms are sweeping through the Midwest, disrupting flights at Chicago airports and knocking out power to hundreds of thousands of customers.

video:video.video:title: Protesters, police clash in Bolivia after president signs law enabling a harsher crackdown
video:video.video:description: Protesters in Bolivia demanding the resignation of conservative President Rodrigo Paz hurled firecrackers, stones and sticks at police who responded with tear gas on Monday, leading to dozens of arrests as road blockades continue to paralyze the Andean nation.

video:video.video:description: Thousands of displaced people and their belongings were seen on Monday heading south in Lebanon, shortly after a tentative deal was announced between the US and Iran.

video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama and former first lady Michelle Obama surprised the first 100 visitors to walk through the doors of Obama’s new presidential center, personally greeting them.

video:video.video:description: Peruvian presidential candidates Roberto Sánchez and Keiko Fujimori voted in Lima on Sunday as the nation went to the polls to decide their ninth president in just 10 years.

video:video.video:title: Helen Mirren leads 'Happy Birthday' song for King Charles at UK embassy in Rome

video:video.video:description: Leaders at the Group of Seven summit on Wednesday threw their support behind U.S. President Donald Trump's tentative agreement with Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz and further extend a shaky ceasefire — even though he's offered scant specificity about how that would be implemented.

video:video.video:description: Michelle Pfeiffer, who stars in 'Margo's Got Money Troubles,' says the show helped her learn more about the online platform.

video:video.video:title: Peruvians are soon to choose their president, but neither candidate has convinced the electorate
video:video.video:description: Peru has two options when they head to the polls on Sunday: Keiko Fujimori of the Fuerza Popular political party and Roberto Sánchez of the Juntos por el Perú political party, who, out of more than 30 presidential candidates in the first round, were the top two choices.

video:video.video:title: Paris Hilton wants to be a soccer mom
video:video.video:description: Paris Hilton wants to be a soccer mom. She was in the crowd as the U.S. began its World Cup run with a 4-1 win over Paraguay.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang tries to quell fears on future of technology
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang — whose work helped enable artificial intelligence — is stressing in an Associated Press interview that society has no choice but to change in the advent of AI.

video:video.video:title: What you need to know about Strait of Hormuz as Iran and US reach tentative deal
video:video.video:description: The United States and Iran reached an initial agreement early Monday to open the Strait of Hormuz and further extend a shaky ceasefire in the Iran war, potentially allowing desperately needed oil and natural gas to reach the global market.

video:video.video:title: Scotland fans celebrate first World Cup win in 36 years with bagpipes in Boston
video:video.video:description: Scotland fans, accompanied by bagpipers, marched to Fenway Park in Boston on Sunday ahead of "Scottish celebration night".

video:video.video:title: Fox Corporation to buy Roku in a $22 billion deal, boosting streaming reach
video:video.video:description: Fox Corp. has agreed to buy the streaming pioneer Roku in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately $22 billion, including debt. (AP Production: Marissa Duhaney)

video:video.video:title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels claim missile attack on Israel
video:video.video:description: Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels claimed an attack on Israel on Monday and said Israel-affiliated vessels would again be a target in the Red Sea, putting the waterway, as well as the Gulf of Aden and the narrow Bab el-Mandeb Strait connecting them, in danger.

video:video.video:title: UFC brings its trademark mayhem to the White House as President Trump celebrates 80th birthday
video:video.video:description: A massive UFC event took over the White House’s South Lawn over the weekend. The event celebrated President Donald Trump’s 80th birthday and the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence.

video:video.video:title: How to avoid World Cup ticket scams online
video:video.video:description: Looking for a World Cup ticket? The buzz around the soccer tournament now under way across North America might inspire a last-minute search online, but fans should be wary of cyber scammers.

video:video.video:title: Activists protest in Abuja, demand rescue of abducted schoolchildren
video:video.video:description: Nigerian activists and civil society campaigners on Thursday staged a protest in Abuja demanding the rescue of pupils and teachers abducted from schools in Oyo State, as concerns grow over recurring kidnappings targeting educational institutions across the country.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo makes flight home from Tenerife aboard Spanish king's jet after plane problem delay
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo's Iberia charter, due to take him back to Rome after a weeklong visit to Spain, was grounded by a technical problem Friday, prompting Spain's king to offer his private jet instead.

video:video.video:title: A taste of home, yerba mate is a shared bond for many World Cup fans
video:video.video:description: Alongside their flags, jerseys and songs, fans coming the World Cup matches will bring along their yerba mate, a beverage that is ubiquitous in some South American countries and has gone global in other cultures.

video:video.video:title: What to know about Patagonia's lawsuit against drag queen Pattie Gonia
video:video.video:description: The clothing brand Patagonia is suing drag queen Pattie Gonia, both of which are popular climate advocates.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 10
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, June 10th: Trump says Tehran will 'pay the price' for stalled talks; US Inflation hits 4.2% in May; Mass shooting kills 12 and injures 9 in a Johannesburg suburb; Scientists discover a deep whale graveyard.

video:video.video:title: Cambodian Supreme Court upholds incitement conviction of opposition politician in charged case
video:video.video:description: Cambodia’s Supreme Court on Friday upheld the incitement conviction of a prominent opposition politician while suspending the remainder of his sentence, keeping him out of prison but unable to practice politics for years.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian regions near the frontlines are seeing a rise in premature births
video:video.video:description: A rising number of babies are being born prematurely — before 37 weeks of pregnancy — in Ukraine, particularly in regions near the frontlines, where some areas have seen rates nearly double since the conflict started with Russia's invasion in 2022.

video:video.video:title: The Nigerian army frees 360 abducted people in northeastern Borno state
video:video.video:description: The Nigerian army said Sunday it freed 360 people abducted by Boko Haram in southern Borno, in the northeastern part of the country.

video:video.video:title: Children and adults in central Gaza receive psychological support through virtual reality sessions
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in central Gaza were longing for fleeting moments where war and trauma did not exist. Some of them visit the Gaza Medical Technology team for a rare chance to put on virtual reality headsets.

video:video.video:title: US strike on an alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military attacked a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Thursday, killing three people, as the Trump administration wages a monthslong campaign against alleged traffickers in Latin America.

video:video.video:title: SpaceX stock soars in Wall Street debut and makes Elon Musk the first trillionaire, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk became the world’s first trillionaire on Friday after shares of his rocket company SpaceX soared in Wall Street's biggest initial public offering of stock.

video:video.video:title: Knicks fans react after Game 3 NBA Finals loss vs Spurs
video:video.video:description: New York Knicks fans gathered in Manhattan’s Bryant Park stayed optimistic after the basketball team lost Game 3 of the NBA Finals to the San Antonio Spurs 115-111 on Monday night.

video:video.video:title: Honda recalls over 880,000 cars due to rear suspension issue
video:video.video:description: Honda is recalling more than 800,000 vehicles because rear suspension components may fail and cause drivers to lose control, increasing the chances of a crash or injury.

video:video.video:title: Carín León says family taking extra precautions after elevator scare
video:video.video:description: During an interview at the Empire State Building on June 4, 2026, Mexican singer Carín León shared an update on his wife after an elevator accident left her with an ankle injury.

video:video.video:title: India's viral Cockroach Party launches nationwide youth protests
video:video.video:description: India’s viral Cockroach Janta Party launched a nationwide protest campaign Thursday. The group is calling for the education minister's resignation after alleged examination irregularities.

video:video.video:description: Fast jets soared above London's Buckingham Palace on Saturday, leaving a trail of red, white and blue smoke, in a culmination of the king's official birthday celebrations.

video:video.video:title: DHS secretary praises state cooperation at New Jersey ICE facility
video:video.video:description: Markwayne Mullin says if needed, he has a plan to pull CBP officers to help with security at the Delaney Hall facility, but says the state is working to provide security there so it’s not needed right now.

video:video.video:title: Tom Hanks, Tim Allen launch 'Toy Story 5'
video:video.video:description: Joan Cusack, Wallace Shawn and Tony Hale among cast gathering to launch the latest adventure in the much loved franchise, in Los Angeles. (June 10)

video:video.video:title: Lonnie Ali reflects on Muhammad Ali's legacy of compassion 10 years after his death
video:video.video:description: The Ali Center in Muhammad Ali's hometown of Louisville, Kentucky, is sponsoring a “Day of Compassion” this week to promote acts of service and caring.

video:video.video:description: Authorities say a pilot and 11 others were killed when a plane taking people on a skydiving outing crashed and caught fire in Missouri.

video:video.video:description: For Rahm Emanuel, the road to the White House runs through the uphill climbs of rural New Hampshire.

video:video.video:description: Former Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell used one of his first major public appearances since stepping down as chairman to strongly defend the independence of the central bank, saying that could destroy "priceless" credibility.

video:video.video:description: Deadly Israeli strikes were reported in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, as tensions remain high despite U.S.

video:video.video:description: Here are the top stories for Monday, June 15: Trump joins world leaders for G7 Summit in France; Oil shipments will slowly rise after U.S.

video:video.video:description: Prague’s St. Vitus Cathedral had its new organ inaugurated on Monday, giving the 700-year-old building, the largest in the Czech Republic, a proper instrument to accompany religious services and concerts.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump showed off the new Air Force One, a formerly Qatari-owned jumbo jet that has been converted into the official U.S. presidential aircraft.

video:video.video:title: Business in the front, party in the back - Danish championships celebrates much-maligned mullet

video:video.video:description: Senate Democrats slammed President Donald Trump after the president derailed the confirmation process of his own nominee to head the nation’s intelligence agencies.

video:video.video:description: Multiple people were shot Saturday afternoon near a community street festival in Toledo, Ohio, police said. Officers are actively searching for suspects.

video:video.video:title: Iranian football team arrives in Mexico for World Cup preparations
video:video.video:description: The Iranian football team arrived in Mexico amid high security early on Sunday for its World Cup preparations.

video:video.video:title: Kelli O'Hara enjoys looseness of 'Fallen Angels'
video:video.video:description: Kelli O'Hara and Rose Byrne co-starred in the revival of "Fallen Angels" on Broadway. (June 7)

video:video.video:description: Revelers, some in drag, others in colorful outfits or wearing rainbow-colored face paint, marched under sunny skies in Zurich to express their support for LGBTQ rights.

video:video.video:title: Italian foreign minister cancels US trip after Trump's 'serious and offensive' remarks toward Meloni
video:video.video:description: Giorgia Meloni for her part posted a video calling Trump’s claims “completely fabricated” and expressing astonishment that he would invent such things about an ally.

video:video.video:title: Atlantic hurricane season forecast to be milder than normal thanks to El Nino
video:video.video:description: Meteorologists predict a developing El Nino could dampen the upcoming Atlantic hurricane season, but it won't eliminate storms.

video:video.video:description: Life appeared normal in Tehran on Tuesday, while the country's central banks says inflation has reached a level unseen since World War II, amid a shaky ceasefire with the United States and Israel.

video:video.video:description: A train has struck a semi-truck in eastern Iowa, killing one person and injuring another. Poweshiek County Sheriff Matt Maschmann said one person in the truck died, while another with serious injuries was taken to a hospital.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited a Barcelona prison Wednesday, keeping up a tradition of Pope Francis, who often used his foreign trips to minister to those who can’t join in the public celebrations when a pope comes to town.

video:video.video:description: The U.S.-brokered agreement, announced in a joint statement by the U.S., Israel and Lebanon Wednesday, comes after Israeli forces made their deepest incursion into Lebanon in more than a quarter century.

video:video.video:description: Palestinians have been suffering from lack of proper toilets across Gaza's vast tent cities. In their bare-bones tent in southern Gaza, Mostafa Shaaban built his family’s makeshift toilet behind a curtain in a corner.

video:video.video:title: Supporters of Karmelo Anthony demonstrate as teen convicted of murder for stabbing another athlete
video:video.video:description: A jury rejected Anthony’s claims of self-defense during a confrontation with Austin Metcalf in stadium bleachers last year.

video:video.video:description: Prosecutors say the killings of three men on Hawaii's Big Island were “especially heinous, atrocious, or cruel.”

video:video.video:description: Nine-year-old DJ Rinoka is a celebrated techno artist in Japan. She decided to become a DJ at four after watching YouTube videos.

video:video.video:description: Croatian police say that a small plane crashed, killing four people. The plane came down on Thursday near Medulin, a small town on the Istria peninsula, a statement said.

video:video.video:title: Taiwan needs US weapons for self-defense as threat from China grows, diplomat tells AP
video:video.video:description: Taiwan's top diplomat in the U.S. says the island needs American weapons to defend against China's growing threat.

video:video.video:description: Beyond Venice’s crowded canals is a fragile lagoon ecosystem. The scientists and environmental groups working to restore these wetlands say a sharp rise in the number of flamingos may be a sign their efforts are paying off.

video:video.video:description: Constance Zimmer, who stars as Carolyn Bessette's mother in the TV series 'Love Story,' says she's glad 'that side of the story was represented.' (June 1)

video:video.video:description: When a rare but deadly rodent-borne virus struck passengers on a cruise ship and seemed to be spreading, there were no treatments for those who fell ill and no vaccines to protect others.

video:video.video:description: Al-Qaqa Ibn Antar, 30, was climbing the steep walls of the Hardah Dam volcanic crater in the southern province of Dhale on Friday when he lost his grip and fell into the 120 metre (393 feet) crater, according to the Civil Defense Authority, which posted a short video capturing the moment of his fall

video:video.video:title: Polls open in Ethiopia's election, with the ruling party expected to win

video:video.video:description: Thousands of people march in Jerusalem’s annual Pride parade, an event tightly secured by police

video:video.video:description: In remote communities near the Sudan-Central African Republic border, pregnant women who fled Sudan’s war are struggling to access health care as clinics close and aid funding dries up.

video:video.video:title: 6.7 magnitude earthquake hits part of Indonesia, followed by strong aftershocks
video:video.video:description: A 6.7 magnitude earthquake shook part of Indonesia and was followed by strong aftershocks. The initial temblor caused strong shaking lasting more than a minute.

video:video.video:description: A large, burning cross was discovered at a Chicago park on Tuesday afternoon, and police said they are investigating how it ended up there and the motive behind it.

video:video.video:description: In an exclusive interview with The Associated Press, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says the AI age demands new social norms and encourages people to embrace the technology's potential instead of fearing it, insisting it will create exciting "new opportunities."

While waiting for US President Donald Trump, French President Emmanuel Macron and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to arrive for a meeting, G7 leaders struck up a conversation about smoking.

video:video.video:description: The events of June 16, 1976 are now commemorated annually as Youth Day. Fifty years after the uprising, many from South Africa’s “born free” generation, those born after the end of apartheid in 1994, still face inequality, unemployment and poverty.

video:video.video:description: Tony Gilroy, the creator and director behind the acclaimed "Star Wars" spinoff "Andor," accepted a Peabody Award for the series’ second season and reflected on what he hopes to see from the franchise moving forward: “I like to think that they’ll stay ambitious.”

video:video.video:description: A B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff at a U.S. Air Force base in Southern California’s Mojave Desert and burst into flames Monday, killing all eight people aboard, military officials said.

video:video.video:title: House vote fails to extend FISA spy tool as Friday deadline looms
video:video.video:description: The impasse could soon result in limitations on what intelligence the U.S. government can collect abroad just as World Cup games begin in cities around the country and ahead of celebrations for the nation’s 250th anniversary.

video:video.video:title: Indian minister says engagement with Myanmar will continue, as country's leader visits New Delhi
video:video.video:description: India said it will continue engaging with Myanmar after Prime Minister Narendra Modi held talks Monday with the leader of the country’s military-backed government, despite Western sanctions imposed after the military seized power in 2021 (AP video shot by Rishi Lekhi).

video:video.video:title: Picture wall of part of centuries-old Lahore Fort completed
video:video.video:description: Conservation efforts to restore the Picture Wall on the northern section of the centuries old Lahore Fort in Pakistan has been completed after more than a decade of work.

video:video.video:title: Building in General Santos City collapses amid a magnitude 7.8 earthquake in the Philippines
video:video.video:description: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake shook part of the southern Philippines early Monday, causing damage in a key coastal city, knocking down power and sparking a tsunami warning on some regional coasts.

video:video.video:title: In Iowa, water pollution is a health threat that also disrupts summer fun
video:video.video:description: Iowa’s waterways face significant pollution challenges due to agricultural runoff and sewage. Authorities say many lakes and streams do not meet standards for swimming, drinking or aquatic life.

video:video.video:title: A new generation of peregrine falcons fledge from their Sagrada Familia home
video:video.video:description: Two peregrine falcon fledglings have flown from their nests at the top of Barcelona’s famed Sagrada Familia, leaving behind crowds with binoculars, and live stream cameras which have been following their every move.

video:video.video:title: Serena Williams is returning to pro tennis at age 44 after nearly four years away
video:video.video:description: Serena Williams is returning to professional tennis at age 44, accepting a wild card entry into a doubles tournament nearly four years after her last competitive match and fueling speculation about a possible Wimbledon comeback.

video:video.video:title: Sub-Saharan Africa is the deadliest place in the world to have a baby, while its population booms
video:video.video:description: Maternal mortality in sub-Saharan Africa, which has the world’s fastest-growing population, accounts for 70% of global maternal deaths.

video:video.video:title: Highly effective prevention drug arrives in South Africa, which has world's highest HIV burden
video:video.video:description: Growing up witnessing the devastating effects of HIV in her family and community in South Africa pushed Olwam Plaatjie to start using preventive HIV medications three years ago.

video:video.video:title: Tiny pony recovers in a fourth-floor apartment in Croatia
video:video.video:description: A month-old pony named Mile is the newest resident of an apartment building in the northern Croatian coastal town of Rovinj.

video:video.video:title: Video shows UK police handcuffing teen after being stabbed, stirring race and knife crime debate
video:video.video:description: The fatal stabbing of a British teenager who was handcuffed by police while his killer stood nearby erupted into a debate Tuesday about policing, race and knife crime.

video:video.video:title: Doctor treating Ebola patients describes challenges health workers face at home and treatment center
video:video.video:description: On the front lines of the Ebola outbreak, healthcare workers are exposed not only to Ebola in health centers but also to rejection by their communities.

video:video.video:title: What rising UFO curiosity could mean for religion
video:video.video:description: Are they aliens or demons? As public fascination with UFOs becomes more mainstream, experts and people of faith are increasingly grappling with what that might mean for religion on Earth.

video:video.video:title: Trump acknowledges support from allies at new Air Force One unveiling
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump thanked China and Middle Eastern allies for their support during the conflict with Iran on Friday as he unveiled the new Air Force One.

video:video.video:title: Paris Hilton on football creating global unity at FIFA World Cup Opening Ceremony
video:video.video:description: Paris Hilton looks forward to one day becoming a soccer mom and says it's magical the way the sport brings people together across the globe. (June 12)

video:video.video:title: Self Esteem on phones vs faces at gigs
video:video.video:description: Self Esteem says phones at gigs aren’t a problem for her, as she becomes the first artist to open new London concert venue, British Airways Arc.

video:video.video:title: Extended bar hours seek to boost businesses during World Cup
video:video.video:description: Pennsylvania is one of many states signing off on extending bar and restaurant hours during the world’s most-watched sporting event. (AP Video: Tassanee Vejpongsa)

video:video.video:title: UN offers condolences for peacekeeper killed in Lebanon, welcomes ceasefire agreement
video:video.video:description: A Serbian UNIFIL peacekeeper was killed and two others were wounded when a mortar shell struck their location.

video:video.video:title: What's it like to cover a World Cup game as an Associated Press video journalist
video:video.video:description: From packing gear to waking up early to drive across state lines for FIFA accreditations, Associated Press video journalist Ty ONeil shows what its like to cover a World Cup game and protests on the sidelines of the tournament.

video:video.video:title: Thousands continue to protest Trump family-linked resort in Albania
video:video.video:description: Thousands of citizens gathered in Tirana for a 12th consecutive day of protests against a proposed luxury resort linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of U.S.

video:video.video:title: Maine voters share their thoughts on US Senate candidates Graham Platner and Susan Collins
video:video.video:description: Maine voters have shared their thoughts on U.S. Senate candidates of their choice during the primary election on Tuesday. (AP Video shot by Rodrique Ngowi)

video:video.video:title: AP Explains: Impact of Brendan Sorsby's court win on college sports and gambling
video:video.video:description: Texas Tech transfer quarterback Brendan Sorsby has won a temporary injunction against the NCAA over his gambling suspension.

video:video.video:title: A conflict over cattle in Brazil's Amazon highlights tensions for Indigenous peoples
video:video.video:description: In northern Brazil, a debate over cattle and conservation is unfolding on Bananal Island. The situation highlights the challenge of balancing conservation, Indigenous rights, and agribusiness.

video:video.video:title: French-Iranian cartoonist and filmmaker Satrapi of 'Persepolis' fame dies aged 56
video:video.video:description: Acclaimed Iranian-French cartoonist and filmmaker Marjane Satrapi, a prominent advocate for women's rights and author of “Persepolis,” has died at 56, the French presidency said Thursday.

video:video.video:title: UK bans under-16s from using social media apps including TikTok and YouTube
video:video.video:description: Britain will ban children aged under 16 from using a range of social media apps, including Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube, to protect them from harmful content and excessive screen time, Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Monday.

video:video.video:title: Iraq World Cup squad celebrate birth of defender Frans Putros' newborn
video:video.video:description: The Iraq national team celebrated the arrival of defender Frans Putros' newborn son, just a day before opening their World Cup match against Norway.

video:video.video:title: A right-wing backlash surges in Latin America as crime fears fuel Bukele-style crackdowns
video:video.video:description: At the start of this decade, Latin America leaned left as progressive politicians gained power amid public outrage over inequities worsened by the pandemic.

video:video.video:title: Destruction seen inside building in Philippines after 7.8 magnitude earthquake
video:video.video:description: An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines Monday, killing at least 19 people, and injuring over 200 others mostly in damaged buildings and sending a 1-meter (3-foot) tsunami into nearby coasts.

video:video.video:title: Death Cab for Cutie's Ben Gibbard: 'Fans are always right'
video:video.video:description: Death Cab for Cutie frontman Ben Gibbard explains why he paid so much attention to the band's fans when writing the indie group's 11th studio album, "I Built You a Tower."

video:video.video:title: Worshipers in Tehran skeptical US will honor initial deal with Iran to end war
video:video.video:description: Worshipers in Tehran on Friday expressed skepticism about Iran's initial deal with the U.S. to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Dozens brave bad weather in Taiwan to honour victims of Tiananmen crackdown
video:video.video:description: This year marks the 37th anniversary since the Chinese army ran tanks over protesters who gathered at the Tiananmen Square in Beijing to demand for more freedom and democracy.

video:video.video:title: World Cup excitement in Mexico tempered by security concerns
video:video.video:description: As Mexico prepares to host World Cup matches, excitement among fans and residents is being accompanied by questions about security and whether the country is ready for the influx of visitors.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he’s going to NBA Finals to root for the Knicks
video:video.video:description: Calling himself a “big fan” of the New York Knicks and owner James Dolan, President Donald Trump — born in Queens — said he’ll be attending at least one game at Madison Square Garden.

video:video.video:title: Deal is reached to end Iran war, as Trump orders stop to US naval blockade
video:video.video:description: The United States and Iran have reached an agreement to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. This offers relief to the global economy more than three months since fighting began.

video:video.video:title: Albanians hold 10th of protest in Tirana against tourism project linked to Trump's son-in-law
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Albanians on Tuesday marched through the capital Tirana against a tourism development project linked to US President Donald Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner.

video:video.video:title: A look at the hidden treasures of Barcelona's Sagrada Familia, ahead of Pope Leo's visit
video:video.video:description: The unique grandeur of the Sagrada Familia — the stone somehow carved to look lush as flora, the sandcastle-like spires, the color-flooded interior — means that even those who work and worship at Barcelona’s world famous basilica each and every day can still be surprised with some fresh wonder.

video:video.video:title: Historic significance of Juneteenth celebrated by Galveston, Texas residents
video:video.video:description: Residents in Galveston, Texas turned out to celebrate the deep roots of Juneteenth in that city. June 19, 1865 marks the day when the last enslaved African Americans in the Confederacy learned of their freedom there.

video:video.video:title: Rex Heuermann sentenced to life in prison without parole in New York’s Gilgo Beach serial killings
video:video.video:description: A Long Island architect who lived a secret life as the Gilgo Beach serial killer was sentenced to life in prison without parole Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Soccer fans spend time with Tim Howard on World Cup field
video:video.video:description: A few lucky fans stood pitchside with former American soccer player Tim Howard as the U.S. began its World Cup run with a 4-1 win over Paraguay last week.

video:video.video:title: Trump arrives at the Octagon to celebrate 80th birthday with UFC show on White House lawn
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump celebrated his 80th birthday Sunday with cage fighting on the White House lawn.

video:video.video:title: Xi makes rare visit to North korea as he and Kim seek stronger China-North Korea ties
video:video.video:description: China's President Xi Jinping and North Korea's leader Kim Jong Un underscored their commitment to deepen cooperation and rebuild their complicated traditional alliance.

video:video.video:title: Royal family attends Trooping of the Colour parade in London to mark King's official birthday
video:video.video:description: The parade was attended by Catherine, Princess of Wales along with her children, Prince George, Princess Charlotte and Prince Louis.

video:video.video:title: US military disable oil tanker violating blockade in the Gulf of Oman, CENTCOM says
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said Wednesday that it disabled an oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman. The Palau-flagged vessel was suspected of trying to violate the American blockade, the latest action by the Trump administration to try to push Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Ike Barinholtz teases Madonna cameo in season two of ‘The Studio’
video:video.video:description: Speaking at the Peabody Awards, Ike Barinholtz discussed Madonna’s cameo in the upcoming second season of “The Studio," adding, "They wanted to go bigger and better in season two."

video:video.video:title: Trump's new attitude toward allies buoyed by their praise for Iran deal
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump isn't fond of international meetings of world leaders. But he changed his tune at the G7 summit after earning support from his counterparts for a tentative agreement he struck with Iran to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Ecuadorian artisans struggle to preserve traditional hand-woven strainers
video:video.video:description: Artisans in Guangopolo, Ecuador, are struggling to keep the traditional craft of weaving horsehair sieves alive.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 11
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, June 11th: Trump backs off new strikes against Iran; Labor Department reports big jump in producer prices; Trump announces new pick for director of national intelligence; World Cup fever strikes Mexico as tournament begins.

video:video.video:title: Larger-than-life looks at fashion graduate show
video:video.video:description: When it comes to dreaming big - and demonstrating it - few things are on the scale of the graduate fashion show for London arts college, Central Saint Martins. (June 5)

video:video.video:title: Lionel Messi's family pleads for 'humanity' as the Argentina captain's father undergoes treatment
video:video.video:description: Lionel Messi's family pleads for 'humanity' as the Argentina captain's father undergoes treatment

video:video.video:title: Sweden to ban mobiles in schools as part of back-to-books strategy
video:video.video:description: Long championed as a leader in tech and digital adoption, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools from the next academic year as part of a broad reversal on the use of screens in classrooms.
video:video.video:tag: Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development

video:video.video:title: Israeli military strikes Beirut suburbs in the lead-up to anticipated US-Iran deal
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military said it launched strikes on Beirut on Sunday targeting Hezbollah infrastructure, despite ongoing efforts to negotiate an end to the U.S.-Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Soccer legend Beckham gets star on Hollywood Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Soccer legend David Beckham received a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame on Friday. As the World Cup dominates sports headlines, Beckham is largely credited with helping broaden the popularity and reach of the sport in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Rising gas prices could mean pricier summer for US boaters
video:video.video:description: U.S. boaters, like drivers, are feeling the pinch from the Iran war with higher gas costs. (AP video: Mike Householder and Dee-Ann Durbin)

video:video.video:title: Signs of revival of interest in faith among young people in Madrid, as pope visits
video:video.video:description: Traveling to Spain in June and France in September, Pope Leo XIV will find traditionally Catholic but now staunchly secular countries with an abundance of historic churches but few people in the pews.

video:video.video:title: Scenes of anguish as relatives bury Lebanese family killed in Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: Six members of the al-Abdallah family - father Hassan, mother Hanan, and four of their children, aged between 20 and six – died in Monday's air strike that demolished their home in Khiam, along the border with Israel.

video:video.video:title: Only on AP: Israeli ambassador to France says 'choice was Iran's to resume hostilities'
video:video.video:description: Israel's ambassador to France on Monday said the "choice was Iran's to resume hostilities" between Israel and Iran as both sides traded fire for the first time since the U.S. agreed to a ceasefire with Tehran two months ago.

video:video.video:title: Cat steals the show during Romeo and Juliet ballet in Turkey
video:video.video:description: A cat stole the spotlight during a performance by the Imperial Russian Ballet Company, casually sauntering onto the stage as dancers performed a scene from Romeo and Juliet in western Turkey last week.

video:video.video:title: 'Dancing Devils' chase children to their first communion in Venezuela
video:video.video:description: Children and spectators on Thursday were chased by 'dancing devils,' parishioners of the Carayaca church in Tarmas in Venezuela, as they took part in Corpus Christi celebrations (AP Video/Andry Rincon)

video:video.video:title: Lebanon and Israel extend fragile ceasefire, create security zones that exclude Hezbollah
video:video.video:description: In a joint statement released after a fourth round of U.S.-mediated talks at the State Department, the two sides said the ceasefire “is contingent on a complete cessation of Hezbollah fire and the evacuation of all Hezbollah operatives” from areas south of the Litani River, which is roughly 30 kilom

video:video.video:title: Animals at the Brookfield Zoo in Chicago gear up ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: From its red river hogs to its Addax, Ike, to the American bison and its Grevy’s zebra, the animals at the zoo enjoyed soccer-themed enrichment activities last week ahead of the games.

video:video.video:title: Mexico fans gather at foot of Angel of Independence after World Cup win
video:video.video:description: Mexico City's iconic Ángel de la Independencia became the center of celebrations Thursday night after Mexico defeated South Africa 2-0 in the opening match of the 2026 World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong starts to promote its first five-year plan in line with mainland China
video:video.video:description: Hong Kong has launched a public consultation for its first five-year plan in a politically symbolic step that brings it closer to mainland China’s development approach.

video:video.video:title: David Hockney dies at 88
video:video.video:description: Acclaimed British artist, David Hockney, whose paintings of pools shimmering in the Los Angeles sunshine became icons of 20th-century art, has died at 88. (June 12)

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo is greeted by enthusiastic crowds at soccer stadium in Madrid
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited the home of Real Madrid Football Club, the Santiago Bernabéu Stadium, where around 80,000 people burst into wild cheers as he entered the arena in an electric golf cart.

video:video.video:title: Demonstrators protest FIFA policy prohibiting pre-Islamic Revolution Iranian flags into World Cup matches
video:video.video:description: Waving the flags of pre-Islamic Revolution Iran, hundreds of protesters opposed to the current government demonstrated outside SOFI Stadium in Inglewood, California where the team's World Cup matches will be initially played.

video:video.video:title: Russian attack sets fire to centuries-old religious site in Kyiv and kills rescuers in Kharkiv
video:video.video:description: A large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine killed rescue personnel in Kharkiv and four people in the capital Kyiv on Monday as strikes set apartment buildings ablaze and sparked a fire at the Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra, one of the country’s most significant religious landmarks.

video:video.video:title: Polls open in Ethiopia's election which is widely expected to be won by the ruling party
video:video.video:description: Polls opened Monday in Ethiopia in an election that is widely expected to be won by the ruling party.

video:video.video:title: Soccer fans spend time with Tim Howard on World Cup field
video:video.video:description: A few lucky fans stood pitchside with former American soccer player Tim Howard as the U.S. began its World Cup run with a 4-1 win over Paraguay last week.

video:video.video:title: Louisiana residents fill sandbags to prepare for Tropical Storm Arthur
video:video.video:description: Louisiana residents are filling bags to prepare for Tropical Storm Arthur, the first tropical cyclone of the season which is expected to produce 20 inches (50 centimeters) of rainfall in some areas.

video:video.video:title: Archaeologists uncover pieces of history from the Battle of Bunker Hill
video:video.video:description: On the same hill where bakers, butchers and candlemakers battled the most powerful army in the world, veterans of a new generation went on their knees in the dirt – searching for artifacts from the conflict 251 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Socceroos fans feeling positive after Australia wins 2-0 against Turkey in the World Cup
video:video.video:description: Many celebrated on Granville Street in downtown Vancouver where lines stretched from one bar to the next.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US 'must' respond after Iran shot down US Army helicopter near Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump blamed Iran for shooting down a U.S. Army helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday and said the United States must respond to the attack.

video:video.video:title: Israelis and Iranians weigh what comes next as countries appear to pause strikes
video:video.video:description: Israelis and Iranians showed conflicted emotions on Tuesday as the two countries appeared to end the latest exchange of strikes; some expressed optimism, while others remained skeptical about what comes next.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV visits a welfare center in Madrid run by the Caritas charity
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited a welfare center run by the Caritas charity in a deprived area of Madrid on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Damage forces postponement of opening of 'cave' installation on Paris' oldest bridge
video:video.video:description: Parts of the installation, which looks like an inflatable cave, appeared ripped and were seen fluttering in high winds, with poor weather the likely culprit behind the damage, the project's media spokesperson said.

video:video.video:title: Only on AP: Under Notre Dame cathedral, a 'dig of the century' unearths 1,700 years of history
video:video.video:description: Archaeologists are digging beneath Notre Dame Cathedral to explore as far back as Roman Paris from 2,000 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong artist braves police attention with commemoration on Tiananmen crackdown anniversary eve
video:video.video:description: A performance artist on Wednesday sought to display a thin red thread in downtown Hong Kong to remember the victims of Beijing’s 1989 Tiananmen Square crackdown, 37 years on from the event.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US military strike killed leader of Tren de Aragua gang with help from Venezuela
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Friday that a “swift and lethal kinetic” U.S. strike has killed Héctor Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called “the infamous leader” of the Tren de Aragua gang.

video:video.video:title: Three Siberian tiger cubs born at Ljubljana Zoo
video:video.video:description: Zoo staff members are monitoring the cubs’ development through cameras installed inside the enclosure and say that 4-year-old Arisa has adapted well to motherhood.

video:video.video:title: Bill Gates arrives to testify before panel investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files
video:video.video:description: Bill Gates has arrived on Capitol Hill to appear before a congressional panel investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files, becoming the latest powerful figure linked to the disgraced financier to testify.

video:video.video:title: Trump is scrapping $1.8B fund meant to compensate president's allies, Blanche says
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration is scrapping plans to create a $1.8 billion fund meant to compensate allies of the president, the Justice Department's top official said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Starmer hosts Zelenskyy, Macron and Merz for talks in London
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy insisted Sunday that Europe must play a key role in any negotiations to end the war with Russia as he arrived for talks in London with staunch European allies in the wake of a Russian drone strike that killed three people.

video:video.video:title: Hugh Jackman enters his pirate era
video:video.video:description: Hugh Jackman has signed on to play pirate Long John Silver in a new adaptation of "Treasure Island," directed by Ridley Scott.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine drones hit St. Petersburg oil terminal ahead of Putin's visit to city
video:video.video:description: Ukraine says its long-range drones struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, Russia and set it ablaze as the city hosted an annual international economic forum promoted by President Vladimir Putin.

video:video.video:title: Climate change is fueling more extreme weather
video:video.video:description: From stronger hurricanes to longer droughts and heavier rainfall, a warming planet is intensifying weather extremes and making them more frequent. (AP Video: Aya Diab)

video:video.video:title: Donkey therapy helps improve patient wellbeing at French hospital
video:video.video:description: Donkeys are helping psychiatric patients at a hospital in France. A unique therapy programme uses the animals to help people with mental health conditions recover.

video:video.video:title: Boots Riley on the bigger message behind 'I Love Boosters'
video:video.video:description: Boots Riley wants "I Love Boosters" to spark discussions around "how the working class can have power."

video:video.video:title: UK stabbing conviction prompts unrest in coastal town, and sparks fierce political debate
video:video.video:description: Police were pelted with chairs, cans, rocks and flares late Tuesday by some of the hundreds who attended a protest in the southern English coast city of Southampton, where Henry Nowak was killed in December.

video:video.video:title: Pottery wheel becomes lifeline for blind war veteran in Ukraine
video:video.video:description: A Ukrainian war veteran who was blinded in combat is now teaching others pottery, a craft he learned during rehabilitation.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang calls on Korean gaming superstar Faker during Seoul visit
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang began his five-day to South Korea on Friday by calling on international gaming superstar Faker.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 8
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, June 8th: Iran and Israel trade weapons fire; Houthi rebels claim missile fired at Israel from Yemen; Powerful earthquake in Philippines; Toledo, Ohio police search for mass shooting suspects.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters battle wildfire that destroyed homes in Spokane, Washington
video:video.video:description: High winds drove a wildfire into a Spokane neighborhood, forcing the evacuation of about 1,200 people and potentially damaging or destroying up to 15 structures, according to fire officials.

video:video.video:title: Strong winds damage buildings and tear trees in Wisconsin as US Midwest braces for severe storms
video:video.video:description: Aerial footage shows cleanup crew cleaning up debris, roofs torn off buildings and trees split in half after a strong storm swept through a Wisconsin city.

video:video.video:title: Orphaned children in Gaza put a huge strain on those left to care for them
video:video.video:description: As the death toll in Gaza surpassed 73,000 on Sunday, the overwhelming number of orphaned children who have lost one or both parents have put a huge strain on those left to care for them.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese Americans do what they can to assist friends, family suffering due to attacks on homeland
video:video.video:description: Lebanese Americans pray, protest and raise money to help friends and family impacted by the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah. (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Protests continue in Albania over tourism project linked to Trump’s son-in-law
video:video.video:description: Protests continued in Albania, Monday, against a tourism project linked to U.S. President Donald Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner. (AP Video shot by Eri Xhabafti)

video:video.video:title: Police: Suspect who took 10 people hostage in California standoff has been shot and killed
video:video.video:description: A man held 10 people hostage inside a California office building before the FBI shot and killed him early Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Dallas Stadium unveils World Cup transformation ahead of opening matches
video:video.video:description: Dallas Stadium will host the first North Texas World Cup match on June 14th between the Netherlands and Japan.

video:video.video:title: Oz says he trusts Trump’s judgement when asked why Pulte is qualified for DNI
video:video.video:description: Mehmet Oz, head of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, had his turn at the White House briefing room podium but had little to offer when reporters asked about Bill Pulte's nomination as acting director of national intelligence.

video:video.video:title: Deadly Russian attack hits Kyiv and damages centuries-old religious site
video:video.video:description: A large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine on Monday killed rescue workers and sparked a fire Monday at one of the country’s most significant religious landmarks, a monastic complex of golden domes in the heart of Kyiv.

video:video.video:title: Brazil fans pack Copacabana Beach for World Cup opener against Morocco
video:video.video:description: Facing pressure to win its first World Cup title since 2002, five-time champion Brazil was outplayed early and needed Vinícius Júnior's 32nd-minute goal to gain a 1-1 draw against Morocco on Saturday in a pulsating, high-profile group match.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese in Tyre and Israelis on border reflect on ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Residents in Lebanon and Israelis along the border on Saturday voiced uncertainty over the interim peace agreement between the Iran-backed Hezbollah militant group and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Author Ann Patchett drives the literary conversation
video:video.video:description: From her bookstore in Nashville, Tennessee, author Ann Patchett is showcasing other literary greats, while continuing her work as a prize-winning novelist with several bestsellers.

video:video.video:title: Armenian PM and opposition vote in election, amid Russian pressure to prevent drift toward West
video:video.video:description: Armenians are voting Sunday in parliamentary elections as the incumbent government, under mounting Russian pressure, seeks to loosen ties with Moscow and deepen cooperation with the West.

video:video.video:title: Díaz-Canel announces economic reforms to attract investment, involve Cubans abroad
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel announced Friday a package of economic reforms aimed at attracting investment, expanding participation by Cubans living abroad in the economy and decentralizing parts of the country’s administration.

video:video.video:title: Meloni slams Trump's claim she 'begged' for a photo with him
video:video.video:description: Italy’s government on Friday slammed U.S. President Donald Trump’s claim that Premier Giorgia Meloni had “begged” for a one-on-one meeting with him during the recent G7 summit, a pushback that suggested the longtime U.S. ally had had enough of Trump’s boasting.

video:video.video:title: The Chinese empress dress trend soaring in Beijing
video:video.video:description: Studios near the Forbidden City in Beijing are transforming visitors into empresses from ancient China as young people explore a revival of traditional culture.

video:video.video:title: Teen dies after being thrown from horse-drawn carriage in New York’s Central Park
video:video.video:description: A teenager died after being thrown to the ground when a Central Park carriage horse bolted away from its driver.

video:video.video:title: Netanyahu vows Israeli forces will maintain occupied 'security zone' in southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Thursday that Israel's military will stay in southern Lebanon, where it has occupied up to 10 kilometers (six miles) from the border.

video:video.video:title: Luigi Mangione will assert psychiatric defense in murder case in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing
video:video.video:description: Luigi Mangione will assert a psychiatric defense at his state murder trial in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

video:video.video:title: Mayhem mars euphoria as New York City celebrates the Knicks' first championship in 53 years
video:video.video:description: Outside Madison Square Garden, a crowd watching on a big screen roared as the Knicks rallied from a 16-point deficit to beat the Spurs in San Antonio in Game 5 of the NBA Finals.

video:video.video:title: Emergency flood warning issued as Tropical Storm Jangmi makes landfall in western Japan
video:video.video:description: A tropical storm is dumping heavy rain and raising flood risks around east-central Japan as it moved into the heavily populated Tokyo region.

video:video.video:description: Spanish hotel chain Meliá has joined a growing list of companies with a long-standing presence in Cuba that are withdrawing or limiting their presence on the island.

video:video.video:description: Julia Louis-Dreyfus says she's inspired by fellow actor June Squibb. (June 7)

video:video.video:description: Business in the front, party in the back - a packed Danish crowd Saturday celebrated the much-maligned but enduring mullet hairstyle at the country’s annual Mullet Championships.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the deal between Iran and the U.S.
